title: Self-Supervised Tyre-Energy Teacher Model As A Feature Generator
summary: Mercedes/Imperial’s tyre-energy paper argues that tyre energy is a strategic latent quantity upstream of pit timing.
rationale: Mercedes/Imperial’s tyre-energy paper argues that tyre energy is a strategic latent quantity upstream of pit timing. Even without telemetry, a surrogate teacher can be learned from public data by predicting near-future pace loss or degradation acceleration, then distilled into the pit classifier.
implementation_hint: Create OOF surrogate targets such as next-2/3-lap cumulative `LapTime_Delta`, future degradation gain, or remaining useful stint length. Train a separate regressor on current-row features only, generate OOF/test predictions, and use those predictions as compact teacher features in the main pit model.
expected_effect: Can inject multi-lap sequence signal without switching the whole pipeline to a sequence model, and may be more stable than directly hand-crafting many higher-order lag features.
risk: If the surrogate target is weakly coupled to actual pit decisions, the extra model adds noise and correlation without lift.
